sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '1992-09-15', DATE '1992-11-14', INTERVAL '0 days' + INTERVAL 1 MONTH);

SELECT id, date_diff('month', start_date, end_date) AS month_diff, start_date + INTERVAL date_diff('month', start_date, end_date) HOUR AS adjusted_start FROM test_dates WHERE adjusted_start > DATE '1992-10-01';
CREATE TABLE interval_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIME, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO interval_test VALUES (1, TIME '01:00:00', INTERVAL '3 hours'), (2, TIME '02:00:00', INTERVAL NULL);
SELECT it.id, it.event_time + (SELECT MAX(duration) FROM interval_test WHERE duration IS NOT NULL) AS new_time FROM interval_test it WHERE it.duration IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE boundary_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, start DATE, end DATE);
INSERT INTO boundary_test VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01', DATE '2023-12-31'), (2, DATE '2023-01-01', DATE '2022-12-31');
SELECT id, date_diff('day', start, end) AS day_diff FROM boundary_test WHERE day_diff < 0;
SELECT b.id, date_diff('month', NULLIF(start, end), end) AS month_difference FROM boundary_test b WHERE month_difference IS NOT NULL;
SELECT b.id, date_diff('day', DATE '2023-01-01' + INTERVAL '1 day', end) AS adjusted_diff FROM boundary_test b JOIN (SELECT 1 + 1 AS dummy) d ON d.dummy = 2;
SELECT b.id, date_diff('month', start, start + INTERVAL 1 DAY) AS month_difference FROM boundary_test b HAVING month_difference > 0;
